participants.extracted: Community-dwelling ambulatory adults aged 55 years and older with symptomatic knee osteoarthritis, radiographic Kellgren-Lawrence grade 2 or 3 femorotibial OA (with or without patellofemoral OA), knee pain on most days, body mass index 27 to 41 kg/m2, and a sedentary lifestyle (<30 minutes/week of formal exercise in the prior 6 months). The trial enrolled 454 participants (mean age 66 years; 72% female), and the analysis included 325 participants with paired X-rays and 105 with paired MRI scans.

interventions.extracted: Participants were randomized to three arms. The diet-only group received an intensive diet-induced weight loss program aimed at an average group loss of at least 10% of baseline body weight. This included partial meal replacements (up to two shakes per day), a low-fat, vegetable-rich 500–750 kcal third meal plan, and an initial 800–1000 kcal/day energy deficit with minimum daily calories of 1100 for women and 1200 for men; calorie targets were adjusted over time, and participants attended frequent nutrition education and behavioral sessions with weekly or biweekly weight monitoring. The exercise-only group completed a 60-minute supervised exercise program 3 days per week for 18 months, consisting of aerobic walking, strength training, a second aerobic phase, and cool-down; the first 6 months were center-based, followed by a transition to optional home-based exercise after 6 months. The diet-and-exercise group received both the intensive weight loss intervention and the exercise program.

outcomes.extracted: The trial planned to measure structural and clinical outcomes over an 18-month follow-up period. Radiographic outcomes included change from baseline to 18 months in minimum joint space width (mJSW) and fixed-location joint space width (JSWx = 0.225) on standardized weight-bearing knee X-rays, analyzed quantitatively. MRI outcomes were assessed in a subsample at baseline and 18 months and included semi-quantitative measures of bone marrow lesion (BML) size, BML count, maximum synovitis score, and effusion score using the BLOKS method on the most symptomatic knee. Quantitative MRI cartilage morphometry outcomes at baseline and 18 months included cartilage volume over the aggregate medial tibiofemoral compartment (MT + cMF), medial tibial cartilage, central weight-bearing medial femoral cartilage, and related morphometric indices such as total subchondral bone area, cartilage-covered area, denuded bone area, cartilage surface area, mean cartilage thickness across the total and cartilage-covered bone area, maximal cartilage thickness, and subregional cartilage thickness measures. For the broader IDEA trial context referenced by the paper, symptomatic outcomes included self-reported knee pain, functional ability, and quality of life, with assessments conducted during the 18-month intervention period, though this structural paper focused on the imaging outcomes.
